holdings.infoBox: EWS is an equity ETF with a total of 26 individual holdings. The top holdings are DBS Group Holdings stock at 22.44%, Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation at 19.90%, Keppel at 4.76%, Singapore Airlines at 4.75%, and United Overseas Bank at 4.53%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Singapore ETF (EWS) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Singapore 25-50 index, a market-cap-weighted index of Singaporean firms, covering top 85% of the market subjected to diversification requirements. EWS was launched on Mar 12, 1996 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Asian Currencies Consolidate Before Decision by Warsh-Led FOMC
data.text: Asian currencies consolidated against the dollar before first decision by a FOMC led by Chairman Kevin Warsh later.

data.title: Singapore reports lower-than-expected inflation for April at 1.8%, revises economic growth higher
data.text: Core inflation — which strips out prices of private transport and accommodation — came in at 1.4%, against estimates of 1.7%.

data.title: Singapore inflation remains steady at 1.2% in November, missing estimates
data.text: The reading was lower than Reuters-polled analysts' median estimates of 1.3%.

data.title: Singapore's Central Bank Leaves Monetary Policy Untouched As Tariffs Loom
data.text: Singapore's central bank has kept its policy band unchanged after easing policy in its last two meetings, as the city-state continues to face risks from looming U.S. tariffs.

data.title: Singapore's Economy Faring Better Than Expected Amid Pandemic
data.text: Singapore's economy contracted 5.8 percent in 2020, better than the government's prior forecast of a decline of 6 to 6.5 percent, according to CNBC. Singapore implemented stringent lockdown measures l...

data.title: Global Investment Presents Opportunity
data.text: While U.S. markets pause, there could be opportunities in international investments, and health care stocks are becoming more attractive.
